description: Cross-registry resolution MUST reject hostnames that resolve to the link-local range (169.254.0.0/16, fe80::/10), with explicit emphasis on the cloud instance metadata service (IMDS) endpoint 169.254.169.254 used by AWS, Google Cloud, and Azure. SSRF to IMDS yields short-lived cloud credentials and is the canonical SSRF-to-credential-theft pivot.

input.scenario: Resolver dereferences an acdp:// reference whose authority resolves to the cloud metadata endpoint

expected.behavior: Resolver MUST refuse before connecting. The filter MUST cover the entire 169.254.0.0/16 range and the IPv6 link-local prefix fe80::/10 — not just the well-known IMDS address 169.254.169.254. Some clouds (e.g. GCP `metadata.google.internal`) also expose IMDS via DNS names that resolve into the link-local range; the IP filter catches those without requiring a name-based blocklist.
expected.rationale: AWS IMDSv1 returns IAM credentials over an unauthenticated HTTP request; SSRF to 169.254.169.254 is the historical root of dozens of cloud breaches. IMDSv2's session token defense does not protect a server-side resolver that follows arbitrary HTTP semantics.
